creator: Dancing Earth Reunion
description: Dear Family, In 1988, six University of Missouri students gathered with a mission—to combine our love for music with our love of the Grateful Dead and to share that with as many people as possible. In doing so we had two solid ideas: Politics had no place on our stage, and we wanted a name that did not reflect the Grateful Dead, per se, but rather focused on the elemental aspects of that music. The political philosophy, to us, was simple: Politics divide; music unites. To focus on politics from the stage would be divisive, and our vision was to create an environment within the music that was positive, accepting, joyous, and celebratory. Our hope was that by proxy, the music would unify, thus creating and facilitating a realization of togetherness and fulfillment. Never did we want anyone to feel ostracized, unwelcome, or in contradiction to our message. For good or ill, we settled on the name Blue Dixie. We arrived at that conclusion by combining two elements that the music possessed: Blues-based rock, and the melodic tonal qualities of Dixieland Jazz. Perhaps due to ignorance or white privilege, we never once considered the idea of the Dixie aspect being a reference to the Confederacy, slavery, Jim Crow, racism, or any kind of racial intolerance. We did not possess the skills or insight to see that the name could be taken the wrong way. Over the course of our touring existence, we toyed a time or two with changing the name of the group. But in the end, we decided not to make the change, as we were a touring band that relied on name recognition to stay on the road and build a base of family. In 1995, the band stopped playing regularly, gathering only every so often since then to play reunion shows. Since then, changing the name barely crossed our minds. But now, in the wake of horrendous tragedies befallen African Americans at the hands of the police and other institutions, and in seeing the massive and important shift taking place in this country, we have decided that it is time to let go of the name Blue Dixie. We realize this move may have minimal effect due to our occasional gigs, and we realize the social impact of this decision may also be minimal. However, we have all decided that we cannot remain silent during this time, and although not performing consistently, we can take action, however small, to show our support in the fight for equality and to end systemic racism. We want to thank you, our musical family, for standing by us during our 30+ years of creating this musical environment. It is our hope that you will now stand with us even stronger. We wish to make clear that we do not support any form of racism or bigotry, and we hope that our music will continue to be inclusive to all and send the message that there is joy and celebration in what we do, but also a responsibility to not turn a blind eye to societal inequities. To that end, please welcome our new name: Dancing Earth Reunion.
subject: Dancing Earth Reunion
title: Dancing Earth Reunion
